items.title: SQL Injection
items.summary: Externally influenced input is used to construct SQL without sufficient separation between data and query structure.
verification_checks: Confirm user-controlled input reaches SQL construction or execution.
Confirm the query is built with string concatenation, interpolation, or formatting.
Confirm no prepared statement, parameter binding, or strong type conversion separates data from SQL.
false_positive_signals: The sink uses parameter placeholders with a separate parameter tuple/list.
The value is converted to a strict numeric type before reaching SQL.
The SQL statement is a static literal and user input is not interpolated.
remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries.
Do not concatenate user-controlled strings into SQL.
Run database users with least privilege and avoid detailed SQL errors in responses.

items.title: OS Command Injection
items.summary: Externally influenced input is used to construct an OS command without neutralizing shell metacharacters or separating arguments.
verification_checks: Confirm attacker-controlled input reaches a command execution sink.
Confirm a shell string is built from user input or shell=True is used.
Confirm input validation or argument separation does not remove command metacharacter impact.
false_positive_signals: subprocess is called with a fixed executable and an argument array without shell=True.
The input is selected from a strict allowlist of fixed values.
The sink receives only static strings.
remediation: Prefer library APIs over shell commands.
Use argument arrays with shell=False.
Apply strict allowlists and run command execution in a sandbox with least privilege.

items.title: Path Traversal
items.summary: User-controlled path components can escape an intended directory and access unauthorized files.
verification_checks: Confirm user input controls a filesystem path or filename.
Confirm canonical path checks do not enforce an allowed base directory.
Confirm traversal markers or absolute paths can influence the final path.
false_positive_signals: Path is resolved and checked to remain inside an allowed base directory.
Only fixed file IDs are accepted and mapped server-side.
The filename is reduced to basename and extension allowlisted.
remediation: Map user-visible IDs to server-side file paths.
Canonicalize and enforce an allowed base directory.
Reject absolute paths, traversal markers, and unexpected extensions.

items.title: Cross-Site Scripting
items.summary: Untrusted input is included in web output without context-appropriate encoding, allowing script execution in a browser.
verification_checks: Confirm untrusted input reaches HTML/JS/attribute output.
Confirm output encoding matches the browser context.
Confirm framework auto-escaping has not been disabled.
false_positive_signals: Template engine auto-escaping is active for the sink.
Trusted sanitizer such as DOMPurify is applied with safe configuration.
The value is rendered as text content, not HTML or script context.
remediation: Use context-aware output encoding.
Avoid raw HTML sinks for user-controlled data.
Use a maintained sanitizer for intentional rich text.

items.title: Server-Side Request Forgery
items.summary: The application fetches a user-controlled URL, allowing requests to internal or unintended network resources.
verification_checks: Confirm user input controls URL, host, or scheme of a server-side request.
Confirm no allowlist blocks private, loopback, link-local, or metadata addresses.
Confirm redirects and DNS rebinding are considered if network access is allowed.
false_positive_signals: The target host is selected from fixed server-side IDs.
A strict allowlist validates scheme and normalized host after redirects.
Only relative paths are accepted against a fixed base URL.
remediation: Use strict destination allowlists.
Block private, loopback, link-local, and metadata IP ranges.
Disable unsafe redirects or validate each redirected destination.

items.title: Insecure Deserialization
items.summary: Untrusted data is deserialized without validation, allowing object injection or remote code execution.
owasp: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
verification_checks: Confirm untrusted input is passed to a deserialization sink.
Confirm the deserializer can instantiate arbitrary classes or call __reduce__.
Confirm no integrity check (HMAC/signature) protects the serialized blob.
false_positive_signals: Only json.loads / yaml.safe_load is used on the untrusted data.
The serialized data is signed and verified before deserialization.
The input is a trusted, application-generated constant.
remediation: Prefer safe formats (JSON) and safe loaders (yaml.safe_load).
Never deserialize untrusted data with pickle/unserialize/ObjectInputStream.
If unavoidable, sign+verify payloads and restrict allowed classes.

items.title: Hardcoded Secret
items.summary: Credentials or secret keys are embedded directly in source code, exposing them to anyone with code access.
verification_checks: Confirm the literal is a real secret, not a placeholder or example.
Confirm the secret is reachable/used by runtime code.
Assess blast radius: which service/account the secret unlocks.
false_positive_signals: The value is a placeholder (your-key, example, changeme, xxx).
The value is a test fixture never used in production.
It is a public/non-sensitive identifier.
remediation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secret manager.
Rotate any exposed credential immediately.
Add secret scanning (Gitleaks) to CI to prevent recurrence.

items.title: Code Injection
items.summary: User-controlled input is executed as code via eval/exec-style sinks.
verification_checks: Confirm user input reaches eval/exec-style execution.
Confirm no safe evaluator (ast.literal_eval) or allowlist is used.
false_positive_signals: ast.literal_eval is used instead of eval.
The evaluated expression is a fixed literal.
remediation: Avoid eval/exec on user input.
Use ast.literal_eval or a safe parser.
Validate against a strict allowlist.

items.title: Server-Side Template Injection
items.summary: User input is embedded into a server-side template that is then rendered, allowing expression evaluation or RCE.
verification_checks: Confirm user input is concatenated into the template string itself (not just passed as data).
Confirm the engine evaluates expressions like {{7*7}}.
false_positive_signals: User input is passed as template context data, not into the template source.
A sandboxed template environment is enforced.
remediation: Never build templates from user input.
Pass user data as context variables only.
Use a sandboxed template environment.

items.title: XML External Entity (XXE)
items.summary: An XML parser processes external entity references in untrusted XML, enabling file disclosure or SSRF.
verification_checks: Confirm untrusted XML is parsed with external entity resolution enabled.
Confirm DTD/entity processing is not disabled.
false_positive_signals: The parser disables DTDs / external entities.
defusedxml or secure processing is used.
remediation: Disable DTD and external entity resolution.
Use defusedxml (Python) or secure parser config.
Do not accept raw XML where JSON suffices.

items.title: Arbitrary File Upload
items.summary: An application allows uploading files that can be executed or interpreted by the server.
verification_checks: Confirm uploaded filename/extension is user-controlled.
Confirm the file lands in an executable/served location.
Confirm no allowlist or content validation blocks dangerous types.
false_positive_signals: Strict extension allowlist is enforced.
Files are stored outside the web root with random names.
remediation: Enforce an extension/content allowlist.
Store uploads outside the web root, rename randomly.
Never execute uploaded content.

items.title: IDOR / Broken Access Control
items.summary: Authorization is not enforced on object references, letting a user access others' objects by changing an identifier.
verification_checks: Confirm an object reference (ID) is user-controlled.
Confirm no ownership/authorization check ties the object to the current user.
false_positive_signals: An ownership or role check is enforced before access.
The identifier is unguessable and access is additionally authorized.
remediation: Enforce per-object authorization (ownership/role) on every access.
Use indirect, unguessable references where feasible.
Apply deny-by-default access policies.

items.title: Open Redirect
items.summary: User-controlled input is used as a redirect target, enabling phishing and token leakage.
verification_checks: Confirm the redirect target is user-controlled.
Confirm no allowlist restricts destinations to trusted hosts.
false_positive_signals: Only relative paths are allowed.
Destination is validated against a host allowlist.
remediation: Allowlist redirect destinations.
Only permit relative paths or known hosts.
Avoid putting raw user input in Location headers.

items.title: NoSQL / LDAP / XPath Injection
items.summary: User input is used to build NoSQL/LDAP/XPath queries without proper neutralization.
verification_checks: Confirm user input is concatenated into a NoSQL/LDAP/XPath query.
Confirm no parameter binding or escaping neutralizes it.
false_positive_signals: Parameterized query builder is used.
Input is strictly validated/escaped.
remediation: Use parameterized query APIs.
Escape/validate query metacharacters.
Apply least-privilege on the datastore.

items.title: Weak Cryptography / Weak Hash
items.summary: Broken or risky cryptographic algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES, ECB) are used for security-sensitive operations.
verification_checks: Confirm a weak algorithm is used for a security purpose (password, token, signature).
Confirm it is not used for a non-security checksum.
false_positive_signals: The weak hash is used only for a non-security checksum/dedup.
A strong algorithm is actually used.
remediation: Use SHA-256+/bcrypt/argon2 for hashing; AES-GCM for encryption.
Use a CSPRNG (secrets/os.urandom) for security tokens.
Remove DES/RC4/ECB usage.

items.title: Vulnerable/Outdated Dependency
items.summary: The project depends on components with known vulnerabilities (CVEs).
verification_checks: Confirm the vulnerable version is actually installed.
Assess whether the vulnerable code path is reachable in this project.
false_positive_signals: The advisory does not affect the used feature/version.
A patched version is already pinned.
remediation: Upgrade to a patched version.
Add SCA (Trivy/OSV) scanning to CI.
Remove unused vulnerable dependencies.

items.title: Security Misconfiguration
items.summary: Insecure configuration such as debug mode enabled, missing security headers, or overly permissive settings.
verification_checks: Confirm the insecure setting is active in production configuration.
Assess exposure (info leak, cross-origin access).
false_positive_signals: The setting is guarded by an environment check and off in prod.
CORS is restricted to a trusted allowlist.
remediation: Disable debug in production.
Set security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options).
Restrict CORS to trusted origins.
